# Schema generation experiment

**Goal:** prove we can mechanically reproduce arranger's auto-generated GraphQL
schema from raw inputs (ES mapping + arranger-projects extended mapping), as
the highest-uncertainty piece of a future server-first replacement of arranger.

**Scope (sessions 1 + 2):** the entity Node type only â€” e.g. `studyNode`,
`participantNode`. No Connection/Edge/Relay wrapping, no Aggregations type,
no outer wrapper types, no JSON-blob extras (`mapping`, `extended`,
`aggsState`, `columnsState`, `matchBoxState`). Diff target: byte-equality
of every `<entity>Node` block in arranger's SDL vs ours.

## Result

**All 7 entities byte-identical.** Generator unchanged from session 1
(only `run.mjs` was parameterized for the CLI; `extendedMapping.mjs` now
returns `{ map, entityName }` so the entity name comes from the project
doc rather than a hardcoded constant).

| Entity (graphqlField) | es_index | Top-level fields | Node lines | Identical? |
|---|---|---:|---:|:-:|
| study | study_centric | 54 | 58 | âś“ |
| participant | participant_centric | 32 | 36 | âś“ |
| variants | variant_centric | 20 | 24 | âś“ |
| file | file_centric | 39 | 43 | âś“ |
| biospecimen | biospecimen_centric | 48 | 52 | âś“ |
| biospecimen_trees | specimen_tree_centric | 5 | 9 | âś“ |
| genes | gene_centric | 18 | 22 | âś“ |

Session 2 widens the de-risking: volume (5 â†’ 54 fields), naming quirks
(singular `study` / plural `variants` / underscored `biospecimen_trees`),
and `index: false` fields all handled correctly by the same six modules.

Arranger quirks faithfully reproduced from real data:
- `long`/`integer` â†’ `Float` (not `Int`; almost certainly to dodge int64 â†’ JS-number overflow)
- Synthesized `id: ID!` and `score: Int` prepended
- Field order: synthesized first, then scalars alphabetical, then object/nested types alphabetical
- `isArray` flag on extended mapping drives `[Scalar]` wrapping
- Nested/object fields appear singular (`clinical_trials: studyClinical_trials`), not as lists â€” the array-ness is encoded in the Connection sub-type, which we did not generate today
- Sub-type names follow `<entity><FieldNameCap>` pattern; underscores preserved

## How to run

```bash
node experiments/schemaGen/run.mjs <es_index>
```

Examples:

```bash
node experiments/schemaGen/run.mjs study_centric
node experiments/schemaGen/run.mjs participant_centric
```

Reads from `experiments/data/`, writes
`experiments/data/our-sdl/<entityName>.graphql`, prints a field-level diff
vs arranger's SDL and a strict char-by-char equality check.

## Module layout

```
experiments/schemaGen/
  fieldTree.mjs        ES mapping JSON â†’ internal field tree (scalar | object | nested | unsupported)
  extendedMapping.mjs  loads arranger-projects-<project> _search response â†’ { map: dotted-pathâ†’metadata, entityName }
  typeMappings.mjs     ES scalar â†’ GraphQL scalar (with the longâ†’Float quirk)
  buildSchema.mjs      tree + extendedMap â†’ GraphQLSchema (currently: just <entity>Node + Node interface)
  compareSdl.mjs       extract one type block by name + field-level diff
  run.mjs              wire it all together
```

Production target is TypeScript; `.mjs` chosen for fast iteration in the
experiment phase.

## Data sources

All committed under `experiments/data/`:

- `mappings/<es_index>.json` â€” `GET <es>/<es_index>/_mapping` for each of the 7 projects (study, participant, variant, file, biospecimen, specimen_tree, gene)
- `arranger-projects/include.json` â€” full `_search` response on `arranger-projects-include` (7 hits; one per graphqlField)
- `arranger-sdl/include.graphql` â€” arranger's `printSchema(buildClientSchema(introspection))` output
- `our-sdl/<entityName>.graphql` â€” our generated SDL per entity (regenerated by `run.mjs`)

Dump scripts:
- `experiments/sdl-dump.mjs` â€” introspect-and-printSchema against a running arranger
- `admin/dumpArrangerProjects.mjs` â€” uses the existing ES client pattern (`new Client({ node: esHost })`) to fetch the arranger-projects doc as proper JSON (Kibana's panel adds `"""..."""` blocks that break `JSON.parse`)

## What's NOT done (intentionally)

- Aggregations type generation (per-aggregable-field, driven by `extended.active` + `extended.type`)
- Outer wrapper type (`study` containing `hits`/`aggregations`/`mapping`/`extended`/`aggsState`/`columnsState`)
- Sub-Node types for nested fields (`studyClinical_trialsNode`, etc.)
- Connection/Edge/Relay pagination types
- Query operation types (`Root`, `viewer`, `node(id:)`, etc.)
- SQON input type (arranger uses untyped `JSON` for `filters` anyway)
- Resolvers â€” pure SDL generation only

## Outstanding decisions to take to the team

1. **Connection/Edge/Node usage on the frontend.** Do downstream consumers actually query the Relay pagination, or do they only use `hits.edges[].node`? A grep on the frontend will tell us; affects whether we generate the full Relay scaffolding or a simpler `{ total, hits[] }` shape.
2. **JSON-blob extras.** `mapping`, `extended(fields:)`, `aggsState`, `columnsState` are exposed on every entity type. `matchBoxState` already declared disposable. Worth a frontend grep to know which of the rest are actually consumed.
3. **`filters: JSON` vs typed `RootSqon` input.** Arranger uses `JSON`. Typing it is strictly better but breaks byte-parity. Defer until we see whether the rewrite delivers value as-is.

## Next session candidates (no decision yet)

- **Aggregations type generation** (~1â€“2 hr) â€” second piece of generated machinery; closes the de-risking case
- **Outer wrapper + connection scaffolding** â€” start filling in the placeholder sub-types
- **Stop and write the team proposal** â€” package today's result into a "go / no-go" recommendation
